title: Cyclosorus tottus
intro: Cyclosorus tottus, also known as the Indian Brake, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus tottus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cyclosorus tottus include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus tottus is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae. This family belongs to the order Polypodiales within the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies. Further classification places Cyclosorus tottus within the subclass Polypodiidae, and the order Polypodiales is a part of the subclass. This fern's classification contributes to a deeper understanding of its evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
